# filters

```go
import "github.com/lucasassuncao/gopaper/internal/filters"
```

Package filters compiles a category's models.Filter into a form that can be evaluated cheaply against many candidate files.

## Index

- [func ParseSize\(s string\) \(int64, error\)](<#ParseSize>)
- [type Compiled](<#Compiled>)
  - [func Compile\(f \*models.Filter\) \(\*Compiled, error\)](<#Compile>)

<a name="ParseSize"></a>
## func [ParseSize](<https://github.com/lucasassuncao/gopaper/blob/main/internal/filters/filters.go#L151>)

```go
func ParseSize(s string) (int64, error)
```

ParseSize parses a human\-readable size string \(e.g. "10MB", "1.5GB", "256MiB"\) into bytes. KB/MB/GB/TB are decimal \(powers of 1000\); KiB/MiB/ GiB/TiB are binary \(powers of 1024\).

<a name="Compiled"></a>
## type [Compiled](<https://github.com/lucasassuncao/gopaper/blob/main/internal/filters/filters.go#L21-L28>)

Compiled is a validated, ready\-to\-evaluate form of models.Filter. Regex is compiled and size bounds are parsed once, so repeated evaluation against many candidate files doesn't repeat that work.

```go
type Compiled struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Compile"></a>
### func [Compile](<https://github.com/lucasassuncao/gopaper/blob/main/internal/filters/filters.go#L32>)

```go
func Compile(f *models.Filter) (*Compiled, error)
```

Compile validates and compiles f. A nil f compiles to a filter that matches every file.

<a name="Compiled.Matches"></a>
### func \(\*Compiled\) [Matches](<https://github.com/lucasassuncao/gopaper/blob/main/internal/filters/filters.go#L103>)

```go
func (c *Compiled) Matches(name string, info os.FileInfo) bool
```

Matches reports whether a file with the given name passes the filter. info may be nil when NeedsFileInfo reports false.

<a name="Compiled.NeedsFileInfo"></a>
### func \(\*Compiled\) [NeedsFileInfo](<https://github.com/lucasassuncao/gopaper/blob/main/internal/filters/filters.go#L97>)

```go
func (c *Compiled) NeedsFileInfo() bool
```

NeedsFileInfo reports whether the filter has any age/size constraints, so callers can skip the file\-info lookup when it would go unused.
